articles.title: How Project Maven taught the military to love AI
articles.summary: Project Maven, a US military AI system using computer vision on drone footage, enabled the acceleration of military targeting during the Iran assault, striking over 1,000 targets in 24 hours. The system, developed since 2017, represents the military's adoption of AI warfare technology.

articles.title: AirPods, Touch Bars, and the rest of Tim Cook’s legacy
articles.summary: Tim Cook is stepping down as Apple CEO, with John Ternus as his likely successor. This leadership change could bring significant shifts to Apple's product strategy and direction.

articles.title: Musk vs. Altman is here, and it’s going to get messy
articles.summary: Elon Musk filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman, with trial scheduled for April 27th in Oakland. The case alleges fraud but represents a deeper conflict over Musk's ousting from the company he cofounded.

articles.title: Meta’s loss is Thinking Machines’ gain
articles.summary: Meta and Thinking Machines Lab exchange talent bidirectionally. While Meta recruits from the lab, the relationship benefits both organizations through knowledge transfer.

articles.title: ComfyUI hits $500M valuation as creators seek more control over AI-generated media
articles.summary: ComfyUI reached a $500M valuation after raising $30M in funding. The platform empowers creators with greater control over AI-generated media including images, videos, and audio.

articles.title: Google to invest up to $40B in Anthropic in cash and compute
articles.summary: Google plans to invest up to $40B in Anthropic to secure compute capacity amid AI competition. This follows Anthropic's limited release of its cybersecurity-focused Mythos model.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure competing against AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has prompted developers to seek alternatives. Goose, a free open-source AI coding agent by Block, offers similar functionality without subscription costs or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and a coding puzzle. The creative recruitment stunt decoded to a Berghain nightclub algorithm challenge, attracting thousands of engineers.

articles.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
articles.summary: Top university websites including Berkeley, Columbia, and Washington University are serving pornography and malware due to poor subdomain management. Researchers found hundreds of compromised subdomains across at least 34 universities exploited by scammers.

articles.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
articles.summary: Kyber ransomware claims to use quantum-safe encryption via ML-KEM algorithm to protect encrypted files. This marks the first confirmed ransomware family using post-quantum cryptography standards.

articles.title: Microsoft issues emergency update for macOS and Linux ASP.NET threat
articles.summary: Microsoft released an emergency patch for ASP.NET Core to fix a high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2026-40372) allowing unauthenticated attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges on Linux and macOS systems through cryptographic signature verification flaws.

articles.title: Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters
articles.summary: DeepSeek released V4, its new flagship model with improved long-prompt processing capabilities. Like previous releases, V4 is open source, continuing DeepSeek's impact on global AI development.

articles.title: The Download: supercharged scams and studying AI healthcare
articles.summary: Cybercriminals are leveraging generative AI to execute sophisticated scams at scale, including phishing, deepfakes, and vulnerability scans. Organizations struggle to defend against the increasing volume and sophistication of AI-driven cyberattacks.

articles.title: Health-care AI is here. We don’t know if it actually helps patients.
articles.summary: AI tools are increasingly used in hospitals for note-taking, patient record analysis, and medical imaging interpretation. However, research hasn't yet proven whether these accurate tools actually improve patient health outcomes.

articles.title: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use
articles.summary: DeepSeek-V4 introduces a million-token context window that enables AI agents to effectively process and utilize vast amounts of information for complex tasks and reasoning.

articles.title: How to Use Transformers.js in a Chrome Extension
articles.summary: Guide on integrating Transformers.js library into Chrome extensions for running machine learning models directly in browsers without server dependencies.

articles.title: Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super
articles.summary: Gemma 4 VLA (Vision Language Model) demonstrated running on Jetson Orin Nano Super, showcasing efficient AI inference on edge devices with multimodal capabilities.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Microsoft's Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and emerging trends in the field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing efforts.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available for developers and farmers to improve farming practices.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Gemini provides AI-powered tips for organizing your home and digital space, including cleaning schedules, inbox decluttering, and seasonal chore management.

articles.title: Here’s how our TPUs power increasingly demanding AI workloads.
articles.summary: Google's TPUs are designed to handle increasingly demanding AI workloads. A new video explains how these specialized processors power advanced artificial intelligence applications.

articles.title: Elevating Austria: Google invests in its first data center in the Alps.
articles.summary: Google announces its first data center in Kronstorf, Austria, creating 100 direct jobs and expanding its presence in the region.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo enables resilient distributed AI training by decoupling local and global updates, improving fault tolerance and efficiency in large-scale model training across multiple nodes.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to help organizations worldwide implement frontier AI technology and accelerate their digital transformation.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS introduces granular audio tags enabling precise control over AI-generated speech for more expressive audio output.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family and other significant AI developments were discussed.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launches DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance photorealism in video games with potential applications beyond gaming. OpenAI develops a superapp while MiniMax releases M2.7 model.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and ChatGPT's interactive math/science visuals. The podcast covers recent industry news on AI agents and tools.

articles.title: Why AI agents need interaction infrastructure
articles.summary: Enterprises need dedicated interaction infrastructure to coordinate independent AI agents and prevent automation waste. Startup Band raised $17M to build this interaction layer for autonomous corporate systems.

articles.title: How AI models use real-time cryptocurrency data to interpret market behaviour
articles.summary: AI models leverage real-time cryptocurrency data streams to analyze volatile market behavior, interpreting continuous price fluctuations rather than static datasets. Crypto's unpredictable patterns provide valuable training material for AI systems despite increased complexity.

articles.title: The billion-dollar startup with a different idea for AI
articles.summary: Yann LeCun's AMI Labs secured $1 billion in funding to develop modular AI systems focusing on specific use cases, rejecting large language models as the path forward for meaningful long-term AI development.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, advancing debugging for collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance developed Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses challenges in complex indoor environments by handling localization, self-positioning, and path planning.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, creating significant supply chain risks.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for concise answers but reduces their ability to explore alternative solutions on difficult problems, revealing a tradeoff between speed and generalization.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, the sophisticated engineering infrastructure built around them is emerging as the true competitive advantage for AI companies.

articles.title: Meet GitNexus: An Open-Source MCP-Native Knowledge Graph Engine That Gives Claude Code and Cursor Full Codebase Structural Awareness
articles.summary: GitNexus is an open-source MCP-native knowledge graph engine that provides AI coding assistants like Claude with full codebase structural awareness, preventing breaking changes by understanding function dependencies.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation on Deepgram Python SDK for Transcription, Text-to-Speech, Async Audio Processing, and Text Intelligence
articles.summary: Tutorial demonstrating Deepgram Python SDK for transcription, text-to-speech, and text intelligence with both synchronous and asynchronous processing, including advanced features like speaker diarization and sentiment analysis.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation on Microsoft’s OpenMementos with Trace Structure Analysis, Context Compression, and Fine-Tuning Data Preparation
articles.summary: Tutorial on Microsoft's OpenMementos dataset exploring reasoning trace structures, compression efficiency, and fine-tuning data preparation through practical Colab implementation and analysis.

articles.title: Get Spotify's student discount and Hulu for just $6 a month - here's how
articles.summary: Spotify offers college students an exclusive bundle combining Spotify and Hulu for just $6 monthly, providing significant savings on music and streaming services.

articles.title: FCC's router ban expands to portable hotspots - 4 things this means for you
articles.summary: The FCC expanded its router ban to include portable hotspots, making device upgrades harder and raising consumer concerns about compatibility and functionality.

articles.title: I'm automating these 5 unexpected home devices - and life has been so much easier
articles.summary: The author automated five everyday home devices, resulting in a significantly smoother and easier daily life. Home automation proved beneficial beyond typical expectations.

articles.title: Building AI Agents with Local Small Language Models
articles.summary: Building AI agents is now accessible to everyone using local small language models, democratizing technology previously limited to major tech companies.

articles.title: Train, Serve, and Deploy a Scikit-learn Model with FastAPI
articles.summary: FastAPI is a popular lightweight framework for serving machine learning models due to its speed and ease of use. It simplifies the process of training, deploying, and serving Scikit-learn models in production environments.

articles.title: AI Agent Memory Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Stateless AI agents lack memory of previous interactions, operating without context from past calls. The article explains AI agent memory across three difficulty levels.

articles.title: Introduction to Approximate Solution Methods for Reinforcement Learning
articles.summary: Introduction to function approximation techniques in reinforcement learning, exploring various approximation function choices for solving RL problems efficiently.

articles.title: I Built an AI Pipeline for Kindle Highlights
articles.summary: A developer created a local, cost-free AI pipeline that automatically processes Kindle highlights by cleaning, structuring, and summarizing reading data without external services.

articles.title: How to Improve Claude Code Performance with Automated Testing
articles.summary: Learn techniques to maximize Claude Code performance through automated testing strategies. This guide helps developers get the most out of Claude Code capabilities.

articles.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 powered Codex runs on NVIDIA's GB200 infrastructure, enabling advanced AI agents for knowledge work. Over 10,000 NVIDIA employees across departments report significant productivity gains using the new agentic coding application.

articles.title: Tag, You’re It: GeForce NOW Levels Up Game Discovery With Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ Labels
articles.summary: GeForce NOW adds in-app labels for Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ games, improving game discovery. The upgrade helps gamers easily identify available titles within their cloud gaming library.

articles.title: Making Sense of the Early Universe
articles.summary: Article discusses understanding the early universe, exploring fundamental cosmological concepts and observations that shape our knowledge of cosmic origins and structure.

articles.title: Building Workforce AI Agents with Visier and Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Visier and Amazon Quick integrate workforce intelligence with internal policies to help employees make faster, better-informed decisions by connecting employee data with organizational context.

articles.title: Amazon Quick for marketing: From scattered data to strategic action
articles.summary: Amazon Quick connects disconnected marketing tools and data sources, enabling marketers to quickly consolidate insights and take strategic action. It creates a personal knowledge graph that learns priorities and preferences, reducing time spent on data management.

articles.title: Applying multimodal biological foundation models across therapeutics and patient care
articles.summary: Multimodal AI foundation models integrate fragmented healthcare data—genomics, medical imaging, clinical records—to improve disease diagnosis, treatment prediction, and drug discovery. AWS provides a unified platform enabling confident decision-making in personalized medicine.

articles.title: Build with DeepSeek V4 Using NVIDIA Blackwell and GPU-Accelerated Endpoints
articles.summary: DeepSeek launched V4-Pro and V4-Flash models optimized for efficient million-token context inference on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. The V4-Pro features 1.6T total parameters with 49B active parameters.

articles.title: Federated Learning Without the Refactoring Overhead Using NVIDIA FLARE
articles.summary: NVIDIA FLARE enables federated learning without requiring code refactoring, allowing organizations to train AI models on distributed data while maintaining privacy and avoiding data movement constraints.

articles.title: Winning a Kaggle Competition with Generative AI–Assisted Coding
articles.summary: Three LLM agents generated over 600,000 lines of code and ran 850 experiments to win a Kaggle competition, demonstrating how AI-assisted coding accelerates machine learning development.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study explores how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, examining generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030 through developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough developments in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient shaping for cleaner action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand how Large Language Models make decisions through feature attribution analysis, advancing AI interpretability and safety at scale.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses encoders to map objects to images and quantifies measurement quality through noise modeling and information estimation techniques.
